blocks.text: Surah Al-Fatiha is the Opening of the Quran and the heart of every prayer. No prayer is valid without its recitation. It is a dialogue between the slave and the Master, containing the essence of the entire Quran within its seven verses. Recited in every Rakaat, it serves as a constant renewal of our covenant with Allah and the foundation of our spiritual life.

blocks.translationEn: In the name of God, the Lord of Mercy, the Giver of Mercy
Praise belongs to God, Lord of the Worlds
the Lord of Mercy, the Giver of Mercy

blocks.text: The Surah begins with the Basmalah, seeking Allah's blessing and assistance. We then offer Al-Hamd—comprehensive praise—to the Lord and Sustainer of all existence. By repeating Ar-Rahman and Ar-Rahim, Allah emphasizes that His vast and specific mercy is the primary lens through which we should understand our relationship with Him.

blocks.translationEn: Master of the Day of Judgement
It is You we worship; it is You we ask for help

blocks.text: Acknowledging Allah as the Master of the Day of Judgment instills accountability, balancing our hope in His mercy with a healthy awareness of His justice. This leads directly to the central covenant: 'It is You we worship and You we ask for help.' This is a declaration of total reliance and a rejection of all partners besides Him.

blocks.translationEn: Guide us to the straight path

blocks.textUthmani: صِرَٰطَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَنۡعَمۡتَ عَلَيۡهِمۡ غَيۡرِ ٱلۡمَغۡضُوبِ عَلَيۡهِمۡ وَلَا ٱلضَّآلِّينَ
blocks.translationEn: the path of those You have blessed, those who incur no anger and who have not gone astray
blocks.tajweedMarkup: صِرَاطَ الَّذِ{madd}ي{/madd}نَ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ غَيْرِ الْمَغْضُوبِ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَ{madd}ا{/madd} الضَّالِّ{madd}ي{/madd}نَ

blocks.text: The Surah concludes with a profound Dua for guidance to the straight path—the path of those Allah has blessed, not the path of those who earned His anger or went astray. This petition for guidance is the most important request a believer can make, as it determines one's success in both this life and the next.

blocks.textEn: Narrated 'Ubada bin As-Samit:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Whoever does not recite Al-Fatiha in his prayer, his prayer is invalid

blocks.text: The Prophet ﷺ emphasized that the prayer of one who does not recite Surah Al-Fatiha is invalid. This underscores its role as the absolute requirement for any connection with Allah in Salah. It is the key that opens the door of the prayer and the core of our daily interaction with the Divine.

In a Hadith Qudsi, Allah says that He responds to each verse. When you say 'Al-Hamdu lillahi Rabb al-Alamin,' Allah says, 'My slave has praised Me.' Realizing that Allah is responding to you in real-time as you recite each verse is a powerful way to achieve deep Khushu.

When you recite Al-Fatiha, do not rush. Pause after each verse and wait for the spiritual echo of Allah's response. This Surah is your personal conversation with the Divine; treat it with the reverence and focus it deserves, knowing that you are being heard and answered.
